content:
As far as I can tell, KBUILD_RUSTFLAGS cannot be overridden from outside the kernel build (HOSTRUSTFLAGS works for host targets, but RUSTFLAGS doesn't route into KBUILD_RUSTFLAGS the same way)

Is this intentional? In this specific case, I was going to inject --remap-path-prefix entries to make our build reproducible. I might be able to send something upstream to do this instead, but was surprised that I couldn't just inject flags into the build.
KRUSTFLAGS should do it (for built-in and modules).
If that doesn't work, please let me know. (It adds extra flags, rather than overriding all existing ones)

Sorry, you're right, not sure how I missed that.

I'm going to send a separate change to set --remap-path-prefix appropriately upstream as well, since C does the equivalent by default, so we should probably automatically do it.
...weird, C appears to only do it for x86? (Edit: nvm, was misreading, it's done unconditionally)
Oh, it's only done for macros, not debug information in C. Seems odd that they wouldn't have an option for this.

Sorry, you're right, not sure how I missed that.

No worries at all, happy to help when I can!

Seems odd that they wouldn't have an option for this.

(I imagine you already saw this, but in case it helps) Yeah, the document on reproducible builds seem to recommend passing -fdebug-prefix-map in KCFLAGS, i.e. manually.
